name: @forwardimpact/guide
description: Conversational agent that grounds career guidance and quality review in your organization's engineering standard.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: The promotion conversation ended with 'not yet' but no specifics — unclear what evidence would change the answer.
jobs.bigHire: get career guidance grounded in my organization's standards, not generic advice.
jobs.littleHire: ask a specific growth question and get advice grounded in my actual standard.
jobs.competesWith: annual review feedback; mentor conversations; LinkedIn Learning; hoping the next project makes readiness obvious; not pursuing promotion at all

forces.push: Career conversations feel subjective with no shared evidence base.
forces.pull: A clear picture of what's needed to close the gap, grounded in evidence.
forces.habit: Waiting for annual feedback rather than continuously self-assessing.
forces.anxiety: Structured analysis might confirm being further behind than assumed.

jobs.firedWhen: gap analysis recommends areas disconnected from actual promotion decisions; or a reorg changes what gets promoted, making the standard feel like last regime's artifact.

jobs.trigger: Reviewing agent output line by line because there's no objective way to tell whether it meets the organization's quality bar without reading everything.
jobs.bigHire: verify agent work against the organization's standard, not my subjective impression of quality.
jobs.littleHire: get a second opinion on a specific deliverable before approving it.
jobs.competesWith: reviewing every line agents produce; accepting slower delivery as the cost of using agents; trusting output uncritically and catching issues later; not using agents for substantive work

forces.push: Full review of every agent deliverable negates the productivity gain of using agents.
forces.pull: Confident delegation — knowing when agent output meets the standard without reading every line.
forces.habit: Reviewing everything because that's how quality has always been caught.
forces.anxiety: Delegating quality judgment to a standard might miss context-specific issues a review would catch.

jobs.firedWhen: standards-based verification catches fewer real issues than full review; or the team stops using agents for substantive work.
